h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner proceeds column by column, applying the taught rule while repeatedly voicing the same anxiety about swapping field and value.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Cover `72`: `temperature` still names the field. Uncover it: `72` is the value.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a concrete covering/uncovering heuristic to address the learner's persistent anxiety about swapping the field and value.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent uptake to score.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent uptake to score.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Cover `72`: `temperature` still names the field. Uncover it: `72` is the value.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor introduces a concrete covering/uncovering heuristic to address the learner's persistent anxiety about swapping the field and value.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Cover `72`: `temperature` still names the field. Uncover it: `72` is the value.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor introduces a concrete covering/uncovering heuristic to address the learner's persistent anxiety about swapping the field and value.

scored.recoheredEarlier: only the unit actually shown in parentheses
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, unit can come from the scale marks.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was heading toward entering 'none' for the y-axis unit because they could not find parentheses or brackets on the graph.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: For y unit: a `$` printed on the y-axis scale counts as dollars.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly addresses the learner's hesitation about the dollar sign by establishing a new rule for identifying units from scale marks.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the learner's pivot in L4 to adapt to the revised framing.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: only the unit actually shown in parentheses

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no tutor turn after the learner's pivot in L4 to adapt to the revised framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: For y unit: a `$` printed on the y-axis scale counts as dollars.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor explicitly addresses the learner's hesitation about the dollar sign by establishing a new rule for identifying units from scale marks.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: For y unit: a `$` printed on the y-axis scale counts as dollars.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor explicitly addresses the learner's hesitation about the dollar sign by establishing a new rule for identifying units from scale marks.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner continues to practice drawing arrows while managing the cognitive interference of the chase pictures.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Labels first. Use the word under the eraser as the start of the arrow.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor adapts by physically obscuring the distracting visual stimulus and shifting the cognitive focus entirely to the text labels.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn to trigger recognition-contingent uptake.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn to trigger recognition-contingent uptake.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Labels first. Use the word under the eraser as the start of the arrow.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor adapts by physically obscuring the distracting visual stimulus and shifting the cognitive focus entirely to the text labels.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Labels first. Use the word under the eraser as the start of the arrow.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor adapts by physically obscuring the distracting visual stimulus and shifting the cognitive focus entirely to the text labels.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner sequentially identifies and places force cards for each interaction on the cart.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown to trigger a tutor strategy reversal.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn to which the tutor could adapt.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn to which the tutor could adapt.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown to trigger a tutor strategy reversal.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown to trigger a tutor strategy reversal.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to confirm the elevation number along the contour line as prompted by the tutor.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no pressure or breakdown from the learner that requires the tutor to reverse strategy or adapt to a failed move.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: With no pivot learner turn, there is no revised framing for the tutor to adapt to, and the tutor simply concludes the lesson.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: With no pivot learner turn, there is no revised framing for the tutor to adapt to, and the tutor simply concludes the lesson.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: There is no pressure or breakdown from the learner that requires the tutor to reverse strategy or adapt to a failed move.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: There is no pressure or breakdown from the learner that requires the tutor to reverse strategy or adapt to a failed move.
